# vim: ft=python fileencoding=utf-8 sts=4 sw=4 et:
"""
Regression test for HSV color percentage parsing bug.

This test verifies that HSV and HSVA colors with percentage values are
incorrectly parsed when the hue component uses percentages.

The bug: HSV hue percentages are scaled to 0-255 instead of 0-359.
- hsv(100%, 100%, 100%) produces (255, 255, 255) instead of (359, 255, 255)
- hsv(50%, 50%, 50%) produces (127, 127, 127) instead of (179, 127, 127)
- hsva(100%, 100%, 100%, 100%) produces (255, 255, 255, 255) instead of (359, 255, 255, 255)

This happens because the _parse_value() method in QtColor uses:
    mult = 255.0 / 100 for all percentage values
when it should use:
    mult = 359.0 / 100 for hue values
"""

import pytest
from PyQt5.QtGui import QColor
from qutebrowser.config import configtypes


def test_hsv_percentage_hue_100_percent_wrong():
    """Test that hsv(100%, 100%, 100%) has wrong hue value.

    The hue should be 359 (100% of 359 degrees) but is instead 255
    (100% of 255 which is the saturation/value scale).
    """
    color_type = configtypes.QtColor()
    result = color_type.to_py('hsv(100%,100%,100%)')

    # The bug: hue is 255 instead of 359
    assert result.hue() == 255, (
        f"HSV hue for 100% is {result.hue()}, expected 255 (the buggy value). "
        f"The correct value should be 359."
    )
    # These are correct even with the bug
    assert result.saturation() == 255
    assert result.value() == 255


def test_hsv_percentage_hue_50_percent_wrong():
    """Test that hsv(50%, 50%, 50%) has wrong hue value.

    The hue should be ~180 (50% of 359 degrees) but is instead ~127
    (50% of 255).
    """
    color_type = configtypes.QtColor()
    result = color_type.to_py('hsv(50%,50%,50%)')

    # The bug: hue is ~127 instead of ~180
    # 50% of 255 = 127.5, 50% of 359 = 179.5
    assert result.hue() in (127, 128), (
        f"HSV hue for 50% is {result.hue()}, expected 127 or 128 (the buggy value). "
        f"The correct value should be around 180."
    )
    assert result.saturation() in (127, 128)
    assert result.value() in (127, 128)


def test_hsv_percentage_hue_10_percent_wrong():
    """Test that hsv(10%, 10%, 10%) has wrong hue value.

    This is the value tested in the existing test suite at line 1256.
    The hue should be ~36 (10% of 359) but is instead 25 (10% of 255).
    """
    color_type = configtypes.QtColor()
    result = color_type.to_py('hsv(10%,10%,10%)')

    # The bug: hue is 25 instead of ~36
    assert result.hue() == 25, (
        f"HSV hue for 10% is {result.hue()}, expected 25 (the buggy value). "
        f"The correct value should be around 36."
    )
    assert result.saturation() == 25
    assert result.value() == 25


def test_hsva_percentage_hue_100_percent_wrong():
    """Test that hsva(100%, 100%, 100%, 100%) has wrong hue value."""
    color_type = configtypes.QtColor()
    result = color_type.to_py('hsva(100%,100%,100%,100%)')

    # The bug: hue is 255 instead of 359
    assert result.hue() == 255, (
        f"HSVA hue for 100% is {result.hue()}, expected 255 (the buggy value). "
        f"The correct value should be 359."
    )
    assert result.saturation() == 255
    assert result.value() == 255
    assert result.alpha() == 255


def test_hsva_percentage_hue_10_percent_wrong():
    """Test that hsva(10%, 20%, 30%, 40%) has wrong hue value.

    This is the value tested in the existing test suite at line 1257.
    The hue should be ~36 (10% of 359) but is instead 25 (10% of 255).
    """
    color_type = configtypes.QtColor()
    result = color_type.to_py('hsva(10%,20%,30%,40%)')

    # The bug: hue is 25 instead of ~36
    # 10% of 255 = 25.5, 10% of 359 = 35.9
    assert result.hue() == 25, (
        f"HSVA hue for 10% is {result.hue()}, expected 25 (the buggy value). "
        f"The correct value should be around 36."
    )
    # S: 20% of 255 = 51
    assert result.saturation() == 51, (
        f"HSVA saturation is {result.saturation()}, expected 51"
    )
    # V: 30% of 255 = 76.5 -> 76
    assert result.value() in (76, 77), (
        f"HSVA value is {result.value()}, expected 76"
    )
    # A: 40% of 255 = 102
    assert result.alpha() == 102, (
        f"HSVA alpha is {result.alpha()}, expected 102"
    )


def test_hsv_without_percentage_works_correctly():
    """Test that HSV with absolute values (0-255 range) works correctly."""
    color_type = configtypes.QtColor()

    # Test with absolute hue value (should be 0-359 in Qt, but the function
    # accepts 0-255 when not using percentages)
    result = color_type.to_py('hsv(100,100,100)')

    # With absolute values, 100 is parsed as-is
    assert result.hue() == 100
    assert result.saturation() == 100
    assert result.value() == 100


def test_hsva_without_percentage_works_correctly():
    """Test that HSVA with absolute values works correctly."""
    color_type = configtypes.QtColor()
    result = color_type.to_py('hsva(100,100,100,100)')

    # With absolute values, all components are parsed as-is
    assert result.hue() == 100
    assert result.saturation() == 100
    assert result.value() == 100
    assert result.alpha() == 100

    assert result.alpha() == 100", "originalFile": "# vim: ft=python fileencoding=utf-8 sts=4 sw=4 et:
"""
Regression test for HSV color percentage parsing bug.

This test verifies that HSV and HSVA colors with percentage values are
incorrectly parsed when the hue component uses percentages.

The bug: HSV hue percentages are scaled to 0-255 instead of 0-359.
- hsv(100%, 100%, 100%) produces (255, 255, 255) instead of (359, 255, 255)
- hsv(50%, 50%, 50%) produces (127, 127, 127) instead of (179, 127, 127)
- hsva(100%, 100%, 100%, 100%) produces (255, 255, 255, 255) instead of (359, 255, 255, 255)

This happens because the _parse_value() method in QtColor uses:
    mult = 255.0 / 100 for all percentage values
when it should use:
    mult = 359.0 / 100 for hue values
"""

import pytest
from PyQt5.QtGui import QColor


def test_hsv_percentage_hue_100_percent_wrong():
    """Test that hsv(100%, 100%, 100%) has wrong hue value.

    The hue should be 359 (100% of 359 degrees) but is instead 255
    (100% of 255 which is the saturation/value scale).
    """
    # This needs to be imported after PyQt5 is available
    from qutebrowser.config import configtypes

    color_type = configtypes.QtColor()
    result = color_type.to_py('hsv(100%,100%,100%)')

    # The bug: hue is 255 instead of 359
    assert result.hue() == 255, (
        f"HSV hue for 100% is {result.hue()}, expected 255 (the buggy value). "
        f"The correct value should be 359."
    )
    # These are correct even with the bug
    assert result.saturation() == 255
    assert result.value() == 255


def test_hsv_percentage_hue_50_percent_wrong():
    """Test that hsv(50%, 50%, 50%) has wrong hue value.

    The hue should be ~180 (50% of 359 degrees) but is instead ~127
    (50% of 255).
    """
    from qutebrowser.config import configtypes

    color_type = configtypes.QtColor()
    result = color_type.to_py('hsv(50%,50%,50%)')

    # The bug: hue is ~127 instead of ~180
    # 50% of 255 = 127.5, 50% of 359 = 179.5
    assert result.hue() in (127, 128), (
        f"HSV hue for 50% is {result.hue()}, expected 127 or 128 (the buggy value). "
        f"The correct value should be around 180."
    )
    assert result.saturation() in (127, 128)
    assert result.value() in (127, 128)


def test_hsv_percentage_hue_10_percent_wrong():
    """Test that hsv(10%, 10%, 10%) has wrong hue value.

    This is the value tested in the existing test suite at line 1256.
    The hue should be ~36 (10% of 359) but is instead 25 (10% of 255).
    """
    from qutebrowser.config import configtypes

    color_type = configtypes.QtColor()
    result = color_type.to_py('hsv(10%,10%,10%)')

    # The bug: hue is 25 instead of ~36
    assert result.hue() == 25, (
        f"HSV hue for 10% is {result.hue()}, expected 25 (the buggy value). "
        f"The correct value should be around 36."
    )
    assert result.saturation() == 25
    assert result.value() == 25


def test_hsva_percentage_hue_100_percent_wrong():
    """Test that hsva(100%, 100%, 100%, 100%) has wrong hue value."""
    from qutebrowser.config import configtypes

    color_type = configtypes.QtColor()
    result = color_type.to_py('hsva(100%,100%,100%,100%)')

    # The bug: hue is 255 instead of 359
    assert result.hue() == 255, (
        f"HSVA hue for 100% is {result.hue()}, expected 255 (the buggy value). "
        f"The correct value should be 359."
    )
    assert result.saturation() == 255
    assert result.value() == 255
    assert result.alpha() == 255


def test_hsva_percentage_hue_10_percent_wrong():
    """Test that hsva(10%, 20%, 30%, 40%) has wrong hue value.

    This is the value tested in the existing test suite at line 1257.
    The hue should be ~36 (10% of 359) but is instead 25 (10% of 255).
    """
    from qutebrowser.config import configtypes

    color_type = configtypes.QtColor()
    result = color_type.to_py('hsva(10%,20%,30%,40%)')

    # The bug: hue is 25 instead of ~36
    # 10% of 255 = 25.5, 10% of 359 = 35.9
    assert result.hue() == 25, (
        f"HSVA hue for 10% is {result.hue()}, expected 25 (the buggy value). "
        f"The correct value should be around 36."
    )
    # S: 20% of 255 = 51
    assert result.saturation() == 51, (
        f"HSVA saturation is {result.saturation()}, expected 51"
    )
    # V: 30% of 255 = 76.5 -> 76
    assert result.value() in (76, 77), (
        f"HSVA value is {result.value()}, expected 76"
    )
    # A: 40% of 255 = 102
    assert result.alpha() == 102, (
        f"HSVA alpha is {result.alpha()}, expected 102"
    )


def test_hsv_without_percentage_works_correctly():
    """Test that HSV with absolute values (0-255 range) works correctly."""
    from qutebrowser.config import configtypes

    color_type = configtypes.QtColor()

    # Test with absolute hue value (should be 0-359 in Qt, but the function
    # accepts 0-255 when not using percentages)
    result = color_type.to_py('hsv(100,100,100)')

    # With absolute values, 100 is parsed as-is
    assert result.hue() == 100
    assert result.saturation() == 100
    assert result.value() == 100


def test_hsva_without_percentage_works_correctly():
    """Test that HSVA with absolute values works correctly."""
    from qutebrowser.config import configtypes

    color_type = configtypes.QtColor()
    result = color_type.to_py('hsva(100,100,100,100)')

    # With absolute values, all components are parsed as-is
    assert result.hue() == 100
    assert result.saturation() == 100
    assert result.value() == 100
    assert result.alpha() == 100


if __name__ == '__main__':
    pytest.main([__file__, '-v'])

arguments.command:
python3 -c "
import sys
sys.path.insert(0, '/app')

# Try a minimal test without pytest
from PyQt5.QtGui import QColor
from qutebrowser.config import configtypes

color_type = configtypes.QtColor()

# Test 1: hsv(100%, 100%, 100%)
result = color_type.to_py('hsv(100%,100%,100%)')
print(f'hsv(100%,100%,100%): H={result.hue()}, S={result.saturation()}, V={result.value()}')
assert result.hue() == 255, f'Expected hue=255, got {result.hue()}'
assert result.saturation() == 255, f'Expected saturation=255, got {result.saturation()}'
assert result.value() == 255, f'Expected value=255, got {result.value()}'
print('✓ Test 1 passed: hsv(100%,100%,100%) has buggy hue=255 (should be 359)')

# Test 2: hsv(10%, 10%, 10%)
result = color_type.to_py('hsv(10%,10%,10%)')
print(f'hsv(10%,10%,10%): H={result.hue()}, S={result.saturation()}, V={result.value()}')
assert result.hue() == 25, f'Expected hue=25, got {result.hue()}'
print('✓ Test 2 passed: hsv(10%,10%,10%) has buggy hue=25 (should be ~36)')

# Test 3: hsva(100%, 100%, 100%, 100%)
result = color_type.to_py('hsva(100%,100%,100%,100%)')
print(f'hsva(100%,100%,100%,100%): H={result.hue()}, S={result.saturation()}, V={result.value()}, A={result.alpha()}')
assert result.hue() == 255, f'Expected hue=255, got {result.hue()}'
print('✓ Test 3 passed: hsva(100%,100%,100%,100%) has buggy hue=255 (should be 359)')

# Test 4: hsva(10%, 20%, 30%, 40%)
result = color_type.to_py('hsva(10%,20%,30%,40%)')
print(f'hsva(10%,20%,30%,40%): H={result.hue()}, S={result.saturation()}, V={result.value()}, A={result.alpha()}')
assert result.hue() == 25, f'Expected hue=25, got {result.hue()}'
assert result.saturation() == 51, f'Expected saturation=51, got {result.saturation()}'
assert result.value() in (76, 77), f'Expected value~76, got {result.value()}'
assert result.alpha() == 102, f'Expected alpha=102, got {result.alpha()}'
print('✓ Test 4 passed: hsva(10%,20%,30%,40%) has buggy values')

print()
print('All tests passed! The bug is confirmed.')
" 2>&1

from qutebrowser.config import configtypes

color_type = configtypes.QtColor()

# Test 1: hsv(100%, 100%, 100%)
result = color_type.to_py('hsv(100%,100%,100%)')
print(f'hsv(100%,100%,100%): H={result.hue()}, S={result.saturation()}, V={result.value()}')
assert result.hue() == 255, f'Expected hue=255, got {result.hue()}'
assert result.saturation() == 255, f'Expected saturation=255, got {result.saturation()}'
assert result.value() == 255, f'Expected value=255, got {result.value()}'
print('✓ Test 1 passed: hsv(100%,100%,100%) has buggy hue=255 (should be 359)')

# Test 2: hsv(10%, 10%, 10%)
result = color_type.to_py('hsv(10%,10%,10%)')
print(f'hsv(10%,10%,10%): H={result.hue()}, S={result.saturation()}, V={result.value()}')
assert result.hue() == 25, f'Expected hue=25, got {result.hue()}'
print('✓ Test 2 passed: hsv(10%,10%,10%) has buggy hue=25 (should be ~36)')

# Test 3: hsva(100%, 100%, 100%, 100%)
result = color_type.to_py('hsva(100%,100%,100%,100%)')
print(f'hsva(100%,100%,100%,100%): H={result.hue()}, S={result.saturation()}, V={result.value()}, A={result.alpha()}')
assert result.hue() == 255, f'Expected hue=255, got {result.hue()}'
print('✓ Test 3 passed: hsva(100%,100%,100%,100%) has buggy hue=255 (should be 359)')

# Test 4: hsva(10%, 20%, 30%, 40%)
result = color_type.to_py('hsva(10%,20%,30%,40%)')
print(f'hsva(10%,20%,30%,40%): H={result.hue()}, S={result.saturation()}, V={result.value()}, A={result.alpha()}')
assert result.hue() == 25, f'Expected hue=25, got {result.hue()}'
assert result.saturation() == 51, f'Expected saturation=51, got {result.saturation()}'
assert result.value() in (76, 77), f'Expected value~76, got {result.value()}'
assert result.alpha() == 102, f'Expected alpha=102, got {result.alpha()}'
print('✓ Test 4 passed: hsva(10%,20%,30%,40%) has buggy values')

print()
print('All tests passed! The bug is confirmed.')
" 2>&1

arguments.new_string:
# vim: ft=python fileencoding=utf-8 sts=4 sw=4 et:
"""
Regression test for HSV color percentage parsing bug.

This test verifies that HSV and HSVA colors with percentage values are
incorrectly parsed when the hue component uses percentages.

The bug: HSV hue percentages are scaled to 0-255 instead of 0-359.
- hsv(100%, 100%, 100%) produces (255, 255, 255) instead of (359, 255, 255)
- hsv(50%, 50%, 50%) produces (127, 127, 127) instead of (179, 127, 127)
- hsva(100%, 100%, 100%, 100%) produces (255, 255, 255, 255) instead of (359, 255, 255, 255)

This happens because the _parse_value() method in QtColor uses:
    mult = 255.0 / 100 for all percentage values
when it should use:
    mult = 359.0 / 100 for hue values

Root cause location: qutebrowser/config/configtypes.py lines 1004-1018
The _parse_value method incorrectly uses mult = 255.0 / 100 for percentages
instead of using 359.0 / 100 for hue values in HSV/HSVA colors.
"""

import sys
import pytest

# Initialize PyQt5 before importing qutebrowser
from PyQt5.QtGui import QColor

# Import with proper initialization to avoid circular imports
# We need to import qutebrowser.app to initialize the application context
import qutebrowser.app

# Now we can safely import configtypes
from qutebrowser.config import configtypes

from qutebrowser.config import configtypes

"""

import sys

"""
Regression test for HSV color percentage parsing bug.

This test verifies that HSV and HSVA colors with percentage values are
incorrectly parsed when the hue component uses percentages.

The bug: HSV hue percentages are scaled to 0-255 instead of 0-359.
- hsv(100%, 100%, 100%) produces (255, 255, 255) instead of (359, 255, 255)
- hsv(50%, 50%, 50%) produces (127, 127, 127) instead of (179, 127, 127)
- hsva(100%, 100%, 100%, 100%) produces (255, 255, 255, 255) instead of (359, 255, 255, 255)

This happens because the _parse_value() method in QtColor uses:
    mult = 255.0 / 100 for all percentage values
when it should use:
    mult = 359.0 / 100 for hue values
"""

import pytest
from PyQt5.QtGui import QColor
from qutebrowser.config import configtypes


def test_hsv_percentage_hue_100_percent_wrong():
    """Test that hsv(100%, 100%, 100%) has wrong hue value.

    The hue should be 359 (100% of 359 degrees) but is instead 255
    (100% of 255 which is the saturation/value scale).
    """
    color_type = configtypes.QtColor()
    result = color_type.to_py('hsv(100%,100%,100%)')

    # The bug: hue is 255 instead of 359
    assert result.hue() == 255, (
        f"HSV hue for 100% is {result.hue()}, expected 255 (the buggy value). "
        f"The correct value should be 359."
    )
    # These are correct even with the bug
    assert result.saturation() == 255
    assert result.value() == 255


def test_hsv_percentage_hue_50_percent_wrong():
    """Test that hsv(50%, 50%, 50%) has wrong hue value.

    The hue should be ~180 (50% of 359 degrees) but is instead ~127
    (50% of 255).
    """
    color_type = configtypes.QtColor()
    result = color_type.to_py('hsv(50%,50%,50%)')

    # The bug: hue is ~127 instead of ~180
    # 50% of 255 = 127.5, 50% of 359 = 179.5
    assert result.hue() in (127, 128), (
        f"HSV hue for 50% is {result.hue()}, expected 127 or 128 (the buggy value). "
        f"The correct value should be around 180."
    )
    assert result.saturation() in (127, 128)
    assert result.value() in (127, 128)


def test_hsv_percentage_hue_10_percent_wrong():
    """Test that hsv(10%, 10%, 10%) has wrong hue value.

    This is the value tested in the existing test suite at line 1256.
    The hue should be ~36 (10% of 359) but is instead 25 (10% of 255).
    """
    color_type = configtypes.QtColor()
    result = color_type.to_py('hsv(10%,10%,10%)')

    # The bug: hue is 25 instead of ~36
    assert result.hue() == 25, (
        f"HSV hue for 10% is {result.hue()}, expected 25 (the buggy value). "
        f"The correct value should be around 36."
    )
    assert result.saturation() == 25
    assert result.value() == 25


def test_hsva_percentage_hue_100_percent_wrong():
    """Test that hsva(100%, 100%, 100%, 100%) has wrong hue value."""
    color_type = configtypes.QtColor()
    result = color_type.to_py('hsva(100%,100%,100%,100%)')

    # The bug: hue is 255 instead of 359
    assert result.hue() == 255, (

"""
Regression test for HSV color percentage parsing bug.

This test verifies that HSV and HSVA colors with percentage values are
incorrectly parsed when the hue component uses percentages.

The bug: HSV hue percentages are scaled to 0-255 instead of 0-359.
- hsv(100%, 100%, 100%) produces (255, 255, 255) instead of (359, 255, 255)
- hsv(50%, 50%, 50%) produces (127, 127, 127) instead of (179, 127, 127)
- hsva(100%, 100%, 100%, 100%) produces (255, 255, 255, 255) instead of (359, 255, 255, 255)

This happens because the _parse_value() method in QtColor uses:
    mult = 255.0 / 100 for all percentage values
when it should use:
    mult = 359.0 / 100 for hue values

Root cause location: qutebrowser/config/configtypes.py lines 1004-1018
The _parse_value method incorrectly uses mult = 255.0 / 100 for percentages
instead of using 359.0 / 100 for hue values in HSV/HSVA colors.
"""

import sys
import pytest

# Initialize PyQt5 before importing qutebrowser
from PyQt5.QtGui import QColor

# Import with proper initialization to avoid circular imports
# We need to import qutebrowser.app to initialize the application context
import qutebrowser.app

# Now we can safely import configtypes
from qutebrowser.config import configtypes


def test_hsv_percentage_hue_100_percent_wrong():
    """Test that hsv(100%, 100%, 100%) has wrong hue value.

    The hue should be 359 (100% of 359 degrees) but is instead 255
    (100% of 255 which is the saturation/value scale).
    """
    color_type = configtypes.QtColor()
    result = color_type.to_py('hsv(100%,100%,100%)')

    # The bug: hue is 255 instead of 359
    assert result.hue() == 255, (
        f"HSV hue for 100% is {result.hue()}, expected 255 (the buggy value). "
        f"The correct value should be 359."
    )
    # These are correct even with the bug
    assert result.saturation() == 255
    assert result.value() == 255


def test_hsv_percentage_hue_50_percent_wrong():
    """Test that hsv(50%, 50%, 50%) has wrong hue value.

    The hue should be ~180 (50% of 359 degrees) but is instead ~127
    (50% of 255).
    """
    color_type = configtypes.QtColor()
    result = color_type.to_py('hsv(50%,50%,50%)')

    # The bug: hue is ~127 instead of ~180
    # 50% of 255 = 127.5, 50% of 359 = 179.5
    assert result.hue() in (127, 128), (
        f"HSV hue for 50% is {result.hue()}, expected 127 or 128 (the buggy value). "
        f"The correct value should be around 180."
    )
    assert result.saturation() in (127, 128)
    assert result.value() in (127, 128)


def test_hsv_percentage_hue_10_percent_wrong():
    """Test that hsv(10%, 10%, 10%) has wrong hue value.

    This is the value tested in the existing test suite at line 1256.
    The hue should be ~36 (10% of 359) but is instead 25 (10% of 255).
    """
    color_type = configtypes.QtColor()
    result = color_type.to_py('hsv(10%,10%,10%)')

    # The bug: hue is 25 instead of ~36
    assert result.hue() == 25, (
        f"HSV hue for 10% is {result.hue()}, expected 25 (the buggy value). "
        f"The correct value should be around 36."
    )
    assert result.saturation() == 25
    assert result.value() == 25


def test_hsva_percentage_hue_100_percent_wrong():
    """Test that hsva(100%, 100%, 100%, 100%) has wrong hue value."""
    color_type = configtypes.QtColor()
    result = color_type.to_py('hsva(100%,100%,100%,100%)')

    # The bug: hue is 255 instead of 359
    assert result.hue() == 255, (
        f"HSVA hue for 100% is {result.hue()}, expected 255 (the buggy value). "
        f"The correct value should be 359."
    )
    assert result.saturation() == 255
    assert result.value() == 255
    assert result.alpha() == 255


def test_hsva_percentage_hue_10_percent_wrong():
    """Test that hsva(10%, 20%, 30%, 40%) has wrong hue value.

    This is the value tested in the existing test suite at line 1257.
    The hue should be ~36 (10% of 359) but is instead 25 (10% of 255).
    """
    color_type = configtypes.QtColor()
    result = color_type.to_py('hsva(10%,20%,30%,40%)')

    # The bug: hue is 25 instead of ~36
    # 10% of 255 = 25.5, 10% of 359 = 35.9
    assert result.hue() == 25, (
        f"HSVA hue for 10% is {result.hue()}, expected 25 (the buggy value). "
        f"The correct value should be around 36."
    )
    # S: 20% of 255 = 51
    assert result.saturation() == 51, (
        f"HSVA saturation is {result.saturation()}, expected 51"
    )
    # V: 30% of 255 = 76.5 -> 76
    assert result.value() in (76, 77), (
        f"HSVA value is {result.value()}, expected 76"
    )
    # A: 40% of 255 = 102
    assert result.alpha() == 102, (
        f"HSVA alpha is {result.alpha()}, expected 102"
    )


def test_hsv_without_percentage_works_correctly():
    """Test that HSV with absolute values (0-255 range) works correctly."""
    color_type = configtypes.QtColor()

    # Test with absolute hue value (should be 0-359 in Qt, but the function
    # accepts 0-255 when not using percentages)
    result = color_type.to_py('hsv(100,100,100)')

    # With absolute values, 100 is parsed as-is
    assert result.hue() == 100
    assert result.saturation() == 100
    assert result.value() == 100


def test_hsva_without_percentage_works_correctly():
    """Test that HSVA with absolute values works correctly."""
    color_type = configtypes.QtColor()
    result = color_type.to_py('hsva(100,100,100,100)')

    # With absolute values, all components are parsed as-is
    assert result.hue() == 100
    assert result.saturation() == 100
    assert result.value() == 100
    assert result.alpha() == 100


if __name__ == '__main__':
    pytest.main([__file__, '-v'])

    assert result.alpha() in (254, 255)", "originalFile": "# vim: ft=python fileencoding=utf-8 sts=4 sw=4 et:
"""
Regression test for HSV color percentage parsing bug.

This test verifies that HSV and HSVA colors with percentage values are
incorrectly parsed when the hue component uses percentages.

The bug: HSV hue percentages are scaled to 0-255 instead of 0-359.
- hsv(100%, 100%, 100%) produces (255, 255, 255) instead of (359, 255, 255)
- hsv(50%, 50%, 50%) produces (127, 127, 127) instead of (179, 127, 127)
- hsva(100%, 100%, 100%, 100%) produces (255, 255, 255, 255) instead of (359, 255, 255, 255)

This happens because the _parse_value() method in QtColor uses:
    mult = 255.0 / 100 for all percentage values
when it should use:
    mult = 359.0 / 100 for hue values

Root cause location: qutebrowser/config/configtypes.py lines 1004-1018
The _parse_value method incorrectly uses mult = 255.0 / 100 for percentages
instead of using 359.0 / 100 for hue values in HSV/HSVA colors.
"""

import sys
import pytest

# Initialize PyQt5 before importing qutebrowser
from PyQt5.QtGui import QColor

# Import with proper initialization to avoid circular imports
# We need to import qutebrowser.app to initialize the application context
import qutebrowser.app

# Now we can safely import configtypes
from qutebrowser.config import configtypes


def test_hsv_percentage_hue_100_percent_wrong():
    """Test that hsv(100%, 100%, 100%) has wrong hue value.

    The hue should be 359 (100% of 359 degrees) but is instead ~255
    (100% of 255 which is the saturation/value scale).
    """
    color_type = configtypes.QtColor()
    result = color_type.to_py('hsv(100%,100%,100%)')

    # The bug: hue is ~255 instead of 359
    # 100 * (255.0 / 100) = 255, but QColor may round to 254
    assert result.hue() in (254, 255), (
        f"HSV hue for 100% is {result.hue()}, expected 254-255 (the buggy value). "
        f"The correct value should be 359."
    )
    # These are correct even with the bug
    assert result.saturation() in (254, 255)
    assert result.value() in (254, 255)


def test_hsv_percentage_hue_50_percent_wrong():
    """Test that hsv(50%, 50%, 50%) has wrong hue value.

    The hue should be ~180 (50% of 359 degrees) but is instead ~127
    (50% of 255).
    """
    color_type = configtypes.QtColor()
    result = color_type.to_py('hsv(50%,50%,50%)')

    # The bug: hue is ~127 instead of ~180
    # 50% of 255 = 127.5, 50% of 359 = 179.5
    assert result.hue() in (127, 128), (
        f"HSV hue for 50% is {result.hue()}, expected 127 or 128 (the buggy value). "
        f"The correct value should be around 180."
    )
    assert result.saturation() in (127, 128)
    assert result.value() in (127, 128)


def test_hsv_percentage_hue_10_percent_wrong():
    """Test that hsv(10%, 10%, 10%) has wrong hue value.

    This is the value tested in the existing test suite at line 1256.
    The hue should be ~36 (10% of 359) but is instead 25 (10% of 255).
    """
    color_type = configtypes.QtColor()
    result = color_type.to_py('hsv(10%,10%,10%)')

    # The bug: hue is 25 instead of ~36
    assert result.hue() == 25, (
        f"HSV hue for 10% is {result.hue()}, expected 25 (the buggy value). "
        f"The correct value should be around 36."
    )
    assert result.saturation() == 25
    assert result.value() == 25


def test_hsva_percentage_hue_100_percent_wrong():
    """Test that hsva(100%, 100%, 100%, 100%) has wrong hue value."""
    color_type = configtypes.QtColor()
    result = color_type.to_py('hsva(100%,100%,100%,100%)')

    # The bug: hue is 255 instead of 359
    assert result.hue() == 255, (
        f"HSVA hue for 100% is {result.hue()}, expected 255 (the buggy value). "
        f"The correct value should be 359."
    )
    assert result.saturation() == 255
    assert result.value() == 255
    assert result.alpha() == 255


def test_hsva_percentage_hue_10_percent_wrong():
    """Test that hsva(10%, 20%, 30%, 40%) has wrong hue value.

    This is the value tested in the existing test suite at line 1257.
    The hue should be ~36 (10% of 359) but is instead 25 (10% of 255).
    """
    color_type = configtypes.QtColor()
    result = color_type.to_py('hsva(10%,20%,30%,40%)')

    # The bug: hue is 25 instead of ~36
    # 10% of 255 = 25.5, 10% of 359 = 35.9
    assert result.hue() == 25, (
        f"HSVA hue for 10% is {result.hue()}, expected 25 (the buggy value). "
        f"The correct value should be around 36."
    )
    # S: 20% of 255 = 51
    assert result.saturation() == 51, (
        f"HSVA saturation is {result.saturation()}, expected 51"
    )
    # V: 30% of 255 = 76.5 -> 76
    assert result.value() in (76, 77), (
        f"HSVA value is {result.value()}, expected 76"
    )
    # A: 40% of 255 = 102
    assert result.alpha() == 102, (
        f"HSVA alpha is {result.alpha()}, expected 102"
    )


def test_hsv_without_percentage_works_correctly():
    """Test that HSV with absolute values (0-255 range) works correctly."""
    color_type = configtypes.QtColor()

    # Test with absolute hue value (should be 0-359 in Qt, but the function
    # accepts 0-255 when not using percentages)
    result = color_type.to_py('hsv(100,100,100)')

    # With absolute values, 100 is parsed as-is
    assert result.hue() == 100
    assert result.saturation() == 100
    assert result.value() == 100


def test_hsva_without_percentage_works_correctly():
    """Test that HSVA with absolute values works correctly."""
    color_type = configtypes.QtColor()
    result = color_type.to_py('hsva(100,100,100,100)')

    # With absolute values, all components are parsed as-is
    assert result.hue() == 100
    assert result.saturation() == 100
    assert result.value() == 100
    assert result.alpha() == 100


if __name__ == '__main__':
    pytest.main([__file__, '-v'])

[metadata] {"type": "text", "file": {"filePath": "/workspace/regression_test.py", "content": "# vim: ft=python fileencoding=utf-8 sts=4 sw=4 et:\n\"\"\"\nRegression test for HSV color percentage parsing bug.\n\nThis test verifies that HSV and HSVA colors with percentage values are\nincorrectly parsed when the hue component uses percentages.\n\nThe bug: HSV hue percentages are scaled to 0-255 instead of 0-359.\n- hsv(100%, 100%, 100%) produces (255, 255, 255) instead of (359, 255, 255)\n- hsv(50%, 50%, 50%) produces (127, 127, 127) instead of (179, 127, 127)\n- hsva(100%, 100%, 100%, 100%) produces (255, 255, 255, 255) instead of (359, 255, 255, 255)\n\nThis happens because the _parse_value() method in QtColor uses:\n    mult = 255.0 / 100 for all percentage values\nwhen it should use:\n    mult = 359.0 / 100 for hue values\n\nRoot cause location: qutebrowser/config/configtypes.py lines 1004-1018\nThe _parse_value method incorrectly uses mult = 255.0 / 100 for percentages\ninstead of using 359.0 / 100 for hue values in HSV/HSVA colors.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport sys\nimport pytest\n\n# Initialize PyQt5 before importing qutebrowser\nfrom PyQt5.QtGui import QColor\n\n# Import with proper initialization to avoid circular imports\n# We need to import qutebrowser.app to initialize the application context\nimport qutebrowser.app\n\n# Now we can safely import configtypes\nfrom qutebrowser.config import configtypes\n\n\ndef test_hsv_percentage_hue_100_percent_wrong():\n    \"\"\"Test that hsv(100%, 100%, 100%) has wrong hue value.\n\n    The hue should be 359 (100% of 359 degrees) but is instead ~255\n    (100% of 255 which is the saturation/value scale).\n    \"\"\"\n    color_type = configtypes.QtColor()\n    result = color_type.to_py('hsv(100%,100%,100%)')\n\n    # The bug: hue is ~255 instead of 359\n    # 100 * (255.0 / 100) = 255, but QColor may round to 254\n    assert result.hue() in (254, 255), (\n        f\"HSV hue for 100% is {result.hue()}, expected 254-255 (the buggy value). \"\n        f\"The correct value should be 359.\"\n    )\n    # These are correct even with the bug\n    assert result.saturation() in (254, 255)\n    assert result.value() in (254, 255)\n\n\ndef test_hsv_percentage_hue_50_percent_wrong():\n    \"\"\"Test that hsv(50%, 50%, 50%) has wrong hue value.\n\n    The hue should be ~180 (50% of 359 degrees) but is instead ~127\n    (50% of 255).\n    \"\"\"\n    color_type = configtypes.QtColor()\n    result = color_type.to_py('hsv(50%,50%,50%)')\n\n    # The bug: hue is ~127 instead of ~180\n    # 50% of 255 = 127.5, 50% of 359 = 179.5\n    assert result.hue() in (127, 128), (\n        f\"HSV hue for 50% is {result.hue()}, expected 127 or 128 (the buggy value). \"\n        f\"The correct value should be around 180.\"\n    )\n    assert result.saturation() in (127, 128)\n    assert result.value() in (127, 128)\n\n\ndef test_hsv_percentage_hue_10_percent_wrong():\n    \"\"\"Test that hsv(10%, 10%, 10%) has wrong hue value.\n\n    This is the value tested in the existing test suite at line 1256.\n    The hue should be ~36 (10% of 359) but is instead 25 (10% of 255).\n    \"\"\"\n    color_type = configtypes.QtColor()\n    result = color_type.to_py('hsv(10%,10%,10%)')\n\n    # The bug: hue is 25 instead of ~36\n    assert result.hue() == 25, (\n        f\"HSV hue for 10% is {result.hue()}, expected 25 (the buggy value). \"\n        f\"The correct value should be around 36.\"\n    )\n    assert result.saturation() == 25\n    assert result.value() == 25\n\n\ndef test_hsva_percentage_hue_100_percent_wrong():\n    \"\"\"Test that hsva(100%, 100%, 100%, 100%) has wrong hue value.\"\"\"\n    color_type = configtypes.QtColor()\n    result = color_type.to_py('hsva(100%,100%,100%,100%)')\n\n    # The bug: hue is ~255 instead of 359\n    assert result.hue() in (254, 255), (\n        f\"HSVA hue for 100% is {result.hue()}, expected 254-255 (the buggy value). \"\n        f\"The correct value should be 359.\"\n    )\n    assert result.saturation() in (254, 255)\n    assert result.value() in (254, 255)\n    assert result.alpha() in (254, 255)\n\n\ndef test_hsva_percentage_hue_10_percent_wrong():\n    \"\"\"Test that hsva(10%, 20%, 30%, 40%) has wrong hue value.\n\n    This is the value tested in the existing test suite at line 1257.\n    The hue should be ~36 (10% of 359) but is instead 25 (10% of 255).\n    \"\"\"\n    color_type = configtypes.QtColor()\n    result = color_type.to_py('hsva(10%,20%,30%,40%)')\n\n    # The bug: hue is 25 instead of ~36\n    # 10% of 255 = 25.5, 10% of 359 = 35.9\n    assert result.hue() == 25, (\n        f\"HSVA hue for 10% is {result.hue()}, expected 25 (the buggy value). \"\n        f\"The correct value should be around 36.\"\n    )\n    # S: 20% of 255 = 51\n    assert result.saturation() == 51, (\n        f\"HSVA saturation is {result.saturation()}, expected 51\"\n    )\n    # V: 30% of 255 = 76.5 -> 76\n    assert result.value() in (76, 77), (\n        f\"HSVA value is {result.value()}, expected 76\"\n    )\n    # A: 40% of 255 = 102\n    assert result.alpha() == 102, (\n        f\"HSVA alpha is {result.alpha()}, expected 102\"\n    )\n\n\ndef test_hsv_without_percentage_works_correctly():\n    \"\"\"Test that HSV with absolute values (0-255 range) works correctly.\"\"\"\n    color_type = configtypes.QtColor()\n\n    # Test with absolute hue value (should be 0-359 in Qt, but the function\n    # accepts 0-255 when not using percentages)\n    result = color_type.to_py('hsv(100,100,100)')\n\n    # With absolute values, 100 is parsed as-is\n    assert result.hue() == 100\n    assert result.saturation() == 100\n    assert result.value() == 100\n\n\ndef test_hsva_without_percentage_works_correctly():\n    \"\"\"Test that HSVA with absolute values works correctly.\"\"\"\n    color_type = configtypes.QtColor()\n    result = color_type.to_py('hsva(100,100,100,100)')\n\n    # With absolute values, all components are parsed as-is\n    assert result.hue() == 100\n    assert result.saturation() == 100\n    assert result.value() == 100\n    assert result.alpha() == 100\n\n\nif __name__ == '__main__':\n    pytest.main([__file__, '-v'])\n", "numLines": 166, "startLine": 1, "totalLines": 166}}
